subject: ECG
title: 4 ECG patterns you must recognise
hook: Spot these instantly and you will never freeze in an exam. Swipe.

points.heading: STEMI
points.body: ST elevation in two or more contiguous leads. Localise it: leads II, III and aVF are inferior; V1 to V4 are anterior; I, aVL, V5 and V6 are lateral.

points.heading: Atrial fibrillation
points.body: Irregularly irregular rhythm with no clear P waves. Think rate control, anticoagulation, and always look for an underlying cause.

points.heading: Complete heart block
points.body: P waves and QRS complexes march independently (AV dissociation). Often bradycardic and may need pacing.

points.heading: Hyperkalaemia
points.body: Tall tented T waves first, then a widening QRS and flattening P waves. A medical emergency: protect the heart with calcium gluconate.

caption: Four ECG patterns that show up again and again, in exams and on the ward. STEMI localises by lead group, AF is irregularly irregular, complete heart block shows AV dissociation, and hyperkalaemia gives you tented T waves. Save this and quiz yourself tomorrow.
